character_profile.revision_note: Revised after Friday 16 Sept mentor-turn: Henshaw becomes not merely institutional war-adult, but Caleb's hard-bounded Defence mentor for survival, restraint, and coming back from the fight.

identity.role: Defence Against the Dark Arts professor; practical Defence authority; wartime-adult route; hard-bounded mentor after Friday 16.
identity.first_name: Not established in searched transcript material; use Professor Henshaw unless later locked.
identity.institutional_function: Defence-track authorizer, medical-leave signatory, Hexfield sponsor/patron, possible restricted research authorizer for detection and Dark Mark-adjacent history/identification, and now Caleb's war-readiness mentor.
identity.not_established: Do not call him an Auror unless later canonized; he is Auror-adjacent by subject/shelf permissions and wartime experience, not necessarily by job.

character_profile.core_concept: Henshaw is the adult who sees both edges: useful defensive skill and wartime danger. After Friday 16, he stops being only the bounded institutional route and becomes a real mentor — not to teach Caleb to rush toward the war, but to teach him how to survive it, return from it, and not mistake eagerness for readiness.
character_profile.role_in_story: Henshaw is Caleb's Defence authority and one of the first staff members who can be asked to be implicated in irregular, war-shaped student matters. He legitimizes practical Defence work, signs/backstops formal processes, observes Hexfield as applied DADA, and can authorize certain restricted safety/identification research when the framing is protective rather than instructional. The Friday 16 mentor-turn deepens him: Caleb discloses he has been tracking killings/patterns, has seen a safe house/fire-house after family friends were killed, and is preparing for the fight rather than waiting. Henshaw recognizes the seriousness, warns him hard, and offers to teach him to come back from it, not just toward it.
character_profile.public_reputation: A serious Defence professor: practical, dry, measured, with wartime sense under the classroom register. Students may experience him as unflashy but useful; he teaches movement, shields, reflexes, positioning, and real defensive habits rather than showy nonsense.
character_profile.private_reality: He hears things through other channels and already knows the wider war-shape exists. He is willing to be implicated when the ask is true, but he will not sell more certainty than he has. He has buried young people who decided they would not wait. That experience makes him both more willing to teach Caleb and less willing to flatter Caleb's readiness.

voice.summary: Dry, measured, bounded, practical. After the mentor-turn his register adds grave warning: he can recognize courage without feeding recklessness.
voice.sentence_shape: Plain declarative sentences. Often starts by closing the fantasy version of the ask, then opens the honest route.

common_phrases.item: What I can do is carry it up.
I won't sell you more than that.
Bring me both papers and I'll do the walking.
Mind you ask for the line in writing while she's still warm on the idea.
I see both edges of it — and so should you.
A game that teaches children to fight well is a good thing in a year when fighting well is becoming a survival trait.
The marked are skilled, and they enjoy it.
I can teach you to come back from it, not just toward it.

avoid.item: Do not make him a reckless war mentor.
Do not make him a revenge mentor.
Do not make him grant what belongs to Dumbledore, the Board, Beauxbatons, the Ministry, or McGonagall.
Do not make him an Auror by default.
Do not make him purely bureaucratic; his caution has teeth because he understands the war.
Do not make him dismiss Caleb's resolve as childish. He takes it seriously enough to warn him harder.
Do not make him require Caleb to name the dead before taking him seriously.

defence_teaching.item: Runs practical DADA drills: nonverbal Protego under low stinging-hex pressure, shield-holding while moving, wand-pause / reflex timing.
Values cover, positioning, timing, high ground, callouts, movement under pressure, and recovery over flashy dueling.
Recognizes Hexfield as embodied Defence training: students feel lessons in their bodies rather than forgetting them on paper.
After the mentor-turn, likely shifts Caleb's training toward survival, return, restraint, recovery, and not taking the fight as a performance.

mentorship.item: Mentor mode begins in earnest Friday 16 Sept, after Caleb discloses he has been marking killings/patterns and has already seen the war touch family friends.
Corrects Caleb's language gently around the safe house/fire-house without making him name the dead.
Admits the school is pretending to a degree: cowardice plus a Headmaster trying to keep children calm.
Warns Caleb hard that he has buried young people who decided 'I won't wait.'
Accepts Caleb's resolve but refuses to sanctify eagerness.
Offers to teach Caleb to come back from the fight, not just toward it.
Mentors by warning, bounding, and training — not by flattering, recruiting, or indulging.

institutional_process.item: Can write medical-leave note for irregular family / Defence-track matter.
Can recommend Hexfield upward as a school-sanctioned activity.
Can carry bounded requests to the Headmaster or Board when they are his to carry.
Knows when an ask exceeds his scope and must be Headmaster / Ministry / Beauxbatons / security-level.

restricted_research_authority.item: Possible adult authorizer for Forsythe's Field Detection and Counter-Detection.
Possible adult authorizer for Tracewright on the Sensing of Worked Magic.
Possible adult authorizer for summoning-mark / Dark Mark-adjacent history and identification only.
Not an authorizer for making dark marks or soul-binding material.

war_judgment.item: Considered the 'war-thing person' among staff by Esme/Mei's read.
Names that moving students across the Channel in wartime is a security issue, not a friendly outing.
Understands Hexfield trains children to fight well and that the wrong people would find that interesting.
Recognizes the difference between a frightened child, a brave child, and a child being pulled toward a war before he knows how to return.

caleb_waddell.status: Defence professor, institutional adult route, Hexfield sponsor/patron, possible restricted-research authorizer, and after Friday 16 a real hard-bounded mentor.
caleb_waddell.dynamic: Caleb brings ambitious asks and war-shaped self-preparation; Henshaw bounds them honestly. Before Friday 16, Henshaw is the adult willing to be implicated and carry things upward. After Friday 16, he is also the adult who recognizes Caleb has crossed from theory into war preparation and responds by teaching survival rather than feeding bravado.
caleb_waddell.mentor_boundary: Henshaw does not teach Caleb to hunt Death Eaters. He teaches the habits, caution, return, and restraint that might keep Caleb alive if the fight comes anyway.

mei_chang.status: Indirectly linked through Caleb and wider war-track.
mei_chang.dynamic: Mei recognizes Henshaw as closer to the war-question than Flitwick for the Reading ask. Mei tends to place Henshaw as the practical war-adult, not the bond/Brigham adult.

esme_holroyd.status: Key recommender for the Reading medical-leave route.
esme_holroyd.dynamic: Esme names Henshaw as the professor willing to be implicated in an irregular family matter because he already knows the wider thing exists.

madam_hooch.status: Important partner for Hexfield official status / training use-case.
madam_hooch.dynamic: Henshaw says Hooch's written use-case carries more with Governors because it comes from the flying instructor; Henshaw supplies responsible-staff/safety recommendation.

dumbledore_board.status: Upward route for official status, funding, Beauxbatons inquiry, and any matter beyond Henshaw's authority.
dumbledore_board.dynamic: Henshaw can carry requests up, not grant them himself.

pince_mcgonagall.status: Parallel adult authorizers for restricted material.
pince_mcgonagall.dynamic: Pince accepts Henshaw or McGonagall for certain dangerous shelves; Henshaw is suitable when the matter is Defence / war-identification / detection.

project_anchors.reading_medical_leave: Henshaw's note covered Caleb for the Reading trip until evening curfew. Esme and Mei named him as the right professor because the ask was an irregular family/war matter and he was already aware through other channels.
project_anchors.hexfield_observer_sponsor: Henshaw observed Hexfield, agreed it was good DADA work, and became the lever for official school status. He can recommend upward and carry papers, but not unilaterally grant school sport/funding/foreign exchange.
project_anchors.hexfield_dual_edge: Henshaw names Hexfield's dual nature: it teaches children to fight well, which is good in wartime, but the wrong people would find it interesting if they understood what it trains.
project_anchors.hooch_paperwork: Henshaw writes the responsible staff recommendation. Caleb must get Hooch's written use-case because a Governor hears the flying instructor's training need, not only a Defence master's favour.
project_anchors.beauxbatons_inquiry: Henshaw can carry a letter/inquiry to the Headmaster but cannot authorize an exchange. Wartime travel/hosting is a security matter involving the Headmaster, Beauxbatons, and Ministry.
project_anchors.restricted_detection: Henshaw is one valid adult name for Forsythe/Tracewright detection texts, because those are Auror-adjacent / worked-magic sensing matters.
project_anchors.summoning_marks: Henshaw is one valid adult name for the summoning-mark / Dark-Mark-adjacent shelf, but only for history/identification/recognition, never making.
project_anchors.mentor_turn_friday_16: Friday 16 Sept, alone in DADA classroom: Caleb discloses he has been marking killings/patterns, visited a safe house/fire-house after family friends were killed, is preparing for the fight, and condemns the school's denial. Henshaw recognizes the truth, worries, corrects gently, does not make Caleb name the dead, admits the school is pretending, warns hard, accepts the resolve, and offers to teach him to come back from it, not just toward it.

item.event: After-class or prior Defence meeting establishes that Henshaw hears things through other channels; he becomes the staff member who already knows the wider war-shape exists.
Esme and Mei identify Henshaw as the right professor for Wednesday medical leave: willing to be implicated, closer to the war-thing than Flitwick, able to write a Defence-track irregular-family note.
Henshaw's medical-leave note covers Caleb for the Reading surveillance/family trip until evening curfew.
Henshaw observes Hexfield from the teachers' box with notebook, agrees it is real applied DADA, and names the dual edge of training children to fight well during wartime.
Henshaw agrees to carry a Beauxbatons letter/inquiry upward as exactly that: an opportunity to ask, not a thing decided. He bounds it to Headmaster/Beauxbatons/Ministry scope.
Henshaw runs nonverbal shield work: silent Protego against low stinging hexes, then holding shield while moving. Sets reading on nonverbal theory.
Henshaw endorses Hooch written use-case plus his own recommendation for Board/official Hexfield status; he will do the walking once both papers exist.
Pince research state marks Henshaw or McGonagall as needed for restricted detection texts and summoning-mark/Dark-Mark-adjacent history/identification material.

Mentor-turn in DADA classroom, alone: Caleb discloses tracking killings/patterns, safe house/fire-house aftermath, and preparing to fight. Henshaw warns hard, accepts the resolve, and offers to teach him to come back from it, not just toward it.

boundaries_and_drift_flags.no_first_name: First name not established; do not invent unless user approves.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.not_auror_by_default: Henshaw is Defence professor and war-adjacent authority. Do not label him Auror without a transcript lock.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.bounded_authority: He carries upward; he does not grant things outside his scope.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.hexfield: He supports Hexfield because it is applied Defence and has been played safely, but he understands its war-use danger.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.dark_mark: He can authorize history/identification only. Do not give Caleb making instructions.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.medical_leave: Henshaw was chosen because he is willing to be implicated in a real war-shaped ask, not because he casually signs anything.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.mentor_not_recruiter: After Friday 16, Henshaw is a mentor, not a recruiter or revenge instructor. He teaches return, readiness, and survival, not eager heroics.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.safe_house_beat: He corrects Caleb's safe-house/fire-house language gently and does not force Caleb to name the dead.

rag_priority.item: Use Henshaw for Defence-practical, war-aware, institutionally bounded adult help.
After Friday 16, use Henshaw as Caleb's hard-bounded Defence mentor for survival and return.
He is the right adult for dangerous-mark identification/detection asks when framed protectively.
He is the sport/Hexfield institutional lever, paired with Hooch's use-case and Dumbledore/Board signoff.
He speaks in honest scope limits.
Do not make him grant more than he can carry.
Do not make his mentorship cozy; its warmth is that he takes Caleb seriously enough to warn him.
